hero.eyebrow: The Wedding Shoe Game — live with all your guests
hero.headline: Two shoes. One question. A room that <accent>holds its breath.</accent>
hero.lead: In the shoe game the couple answers by raising shoes. With TwoSoles the whole room votes first on their phones — and the projector reveals whether the majority really knows the couple.
hero.imageAlt: Wedding couple raising shoes during the shoe game

questions: Who's more likely to give in during an argument?
Who's more likely to stumble during the first dance?
Who takes longer in the bathroom?
Who's more likely to forget the wedding anniversary?

items.label: 5-minute setup
items.title: You set it up
items.description: Enter the couple's names, pick questions or write your own, generate the QR code — done in five minutes, even on the wedding day itself.

items.label: No app download
items.title: The audience votes
items.description: Guests scan the QR code and vote anonymously on their own phones. No app, no login — from the godmother to the second cousin.

items.label: One button press
items.title: The reveal moment
items.description: The couple raises their shoes, you press a button — and the projector reveals how the room voted. The „ohhhh“ comes all by itself.

quote.text: “Finally, something where <accent>everyone joins in</accent> and doesn't just watch.”

pricing.title: How many guests are <accent>coming?</accent>
pricing.lead: One-off per event. No subscription. Small gathering? Completely free.

testimonials.title: Afterwards everyone asks: <accent>“Where did you get this?”</accent>

items.quote: “I set it up two hours before my best friend's wedding. The room completely lost it at the first reveal.”
items.name: Lena M.

items.quote: “140 guests, zero tech problems. QR code on the projector, thirty seconds later everyone had voted. Even our grandma — and she got every question right.”
items.name: Daniel R.

items.quote: “Tested it at the pre-wedding party, played it again at the wedding. Both times the moment of the evening. One question reopened a ten-year debate.”
items.name: Aylin T.

faq.title: Briefly <accent>answered</accent>
faq.note: Didn't find your answer? Contact our <support>support</support>.

items.question: Do guests need an app?
items.answer: No. Guests scan the QR code and vote right in the browser — no install, no login.

items.question: What if the venue's Wi-Fi is bad?
items.answer: Voting runs over the guests' mobile data, not the venue Wi-Fi. A couple of bars of signal is enough.

items.question: Can I add my own questions?
items.answer: Yes. Pick from our templates or write as many of your own as you like — tailored to your couple.

items.question: How far in advance do I need to book?
items.answer: Not in advance at all. You can create your event in five minutes — even on the wedding day itself.

cta.title: The wedding game <accent>people talk about for years.</accent>
cta.lead: Ready in five minutes. No setup, no app for your guests.
cta.imageAlt: Wedding couple celebrating during the game

footer.copyright: © 2026 TwoSoles · Made for best men & maids of honour with a plan.

contact.title: Write to us – we're here for you
contact.subtitle: Whether it's a question about your event, a technical issue or a special idea: we'll help you personally.

info.title: Prefer it direct?
info.lead: You can reach us the classic way too — we read every message ourselves, no bot in between.

bigEvent.title: Planning a big event?
bigEvent.value: More than 300 guests or a special format? Write to us via the form — we'll find a solution.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: Protecting your personal data matters to us. Below we explain which data we process when you use TwoSoles, and why.

sections.heading: Controller
sections.body:
The controller responsible for data processing is:
[Name / company name]
[Address]
Email: [email address]

sections.heading: What data we process
sections.body:
Account data: the email address and name you provide when registering.
Event and game data: the questions you create and the anonymous votes cast by your guests.
Technical data: server log data such as IP address and the date and time of access, processed for technical reasons.

sections.heading: Purposes and legal bases
sections.body: We process your data to provide the service and to perform our contract with you (Art. 6(1)(b) GDPR), to ensure secure operation based on our legitimate interest (Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR) and, where required, based on your consent (Art. 6(1)(a) GDPR).

sections.heading: Services we use
sections.body:
Hosting: [Vercel Inc., USA].
Authentication and database: [Supabase].
Payment processing: [Stripe]. For paid bookings, the data required for payment is transmitted to the payment provider. Where required, data processing agreements are in place with all providers.

sections.heading: Cookies
sections.body: We only use technically necessary cookies, for example to store your login and your language choice. These are required for operation and do not require consent.

sections.heading: Retention period
sections.body: We store your data only for as long as necessary for the stated purposes or as required by statutory retention periods. Afterwards the data is deleted.

sections.heading: Your rights
sections.body: You have the right to access, rectification, erasure and restriction of processing of your data, as well as to data portability and objection. You also have the right to lodge a complaint with a data protection supervisory authority.

sections.heading: Data protection contact
sections.body: If you have any questions about data protection, you can reach us at: [email address].

terms.title: Terms & Conditions
terms.intro: These Terms & Conditions govern the use of the TwoSoles service.

sections.heading: 1. Scope
sections.body: These terms apply to the use of the online services provided via TwoSoles by consumers and businesses. Conflicting terms of the user are not accepted unless their validity is expressly agreed.

sections.heading: 2. Description of services
sections.body: TwoSoles is a digital version of the wedding shoe game. The provider offers an application that lets guests vote live via QR code and presents the results. The specific scope of features depends on the chosen package size.

sections.heading: 3. Registration and conclusion of contract
sections.body: An account is required to use the service. Sign-in is passwordless via a login link sent by email. A contract for a paid package is concluded upon confirmation of the booking.

sections.heading: 4. Prices and payment
sections.body: Use is charged as a one-off payment per event; the price depends on the booked guest capacity. Small events are free. Payment is handled by our payment provider. All prices include statutory VAT.

sections.heading: 5. Right of withdrawal
sections.body: Consumers generally have a statutory right of withdrawal. For digital content, this right may expire early if performance begins, with the consumer's express consent, before the withdrawal period ends. [Add withdrawal instructions.]

sections.heading: 6. Availability
sections.body: The provider strives to keep the service available with as little interruption as possible but does not owe uninterrupted availability. Maintenance, technical faults or force majeure may lead to restrictions.

sections.heading: 7. Liability
sections.body: The provider is liable without limitation in cases of intent and gross negligence and under applicable product liability law. In the case of slight negligence, the provider is only liable for the breach of an essential contractual obligation and limited to the foreseeable damage typical for the contract.

sections.heading: 8. Final provisions
sections.body: The law of the Federal Republic of Germany applies. Should individual provisions of these terms be invalid, the validity of the remaining provisions remains unaffected.

goLiveConfirm.title: Really set the event live?

points: You can set each event live only ONCE.
From going live you have exactly 48 hours to play the game.
After the 48 hours the event is permanently over and can't be started again.

guide.title: How to play TwoSoles
guide.intro: The shoe game, live with the whole room. Plan around 20 to 25 minutes for 10 to 15 questions. Here's the whole flow to read along or download as a PDF.
guide.idea: Every question is a “Who's more likely to?”. There are always exactly two answers to choose from, person 1 or person 2. Everyone in the room taps one of the two on their phone. The couple answers by raising a shoe. The big question at the end of each round: did the audience read the couple right?
needs: A large screen so everyone in the room can see the projector view. That can be a projector with a screen, a big TV or a monitor.
A device you connect to the screen. We recommend a laptop or tablet at least 1024px wide.
A second device for the host controls, which you use to run the game. Laptop, tablet or phone.
An internet connection for the device on the screen and the host device. Guests need internet too. Mobile data is usually enough.
Two chairs and a pair of shoes from each of the two. In the end each person holds one of their own shoes and one of the other person's.
guide.viewsIntro: Before you start, it's worth a look at the three views the game runs across.

views.title: Projector view
views.text: Runs on the screen and is visible to everyone. It shows the welcome screen and the current question.

views.title: Host controls
views.text: Your device, which you use to run the game. Here you open questions, close the vote, reveal, and find your question set and a few settings.

views.title: Guest view
views.text: The game view for guests, which opens on their phone via QR code. It shows the current question and the two answers to choose from.

setup: Connect the first device to the screen and open the <display>projector view</display> on it. It's best to use your browser's fullscreen mode. On a second device, ideally a smartphone, you open the <host>host controls</host>.

Finally, make sure the game is reset so it starts from the beginning. You do this in the settings of the <host>host controls</host>. After that the screen sits on the welcome screen and you're ready to go.

Set up two chairs back to back, ideally in front of or next to the screen. That way the couple sits back to back and can't see each other. Make sure both the couple and the screen are clearly visible to everyone in the room. The couple takes their seats, and each person is handed one of their own shoes and one of the other person's.

round.title: Guests connect.
round.text: A QR code appears on the screen for everyone to see. Guests scan it with their phone camera and land right on the voting page, with no app and no login. Tip: you can also place the QR codes on the tables. Grab the printable PDF <download>here</download>.

round.title: Ask a question.
round.text: You open the first question in the <host>host controls</host>. It appears large on the screen, and guests tap person 1 or person 2 on their phone. In the host controls you see live how many votes are already in.

round.title: Close the vote.
round.text: Once enough votes are in, you close the vote. The screen now shows “Closed. The reveal is coming.”, but the result stays hidden. This is exactly when the couple raises their shoes. Each person raises the shoe of whoever they're voting for. Their own shoe means “me”, the other person's shoe means “you”. Both decide independently and are welcome to disagree. Because the audience result is still hidden, no one can be swayed.

round.title: The reveal.
round.text: You press “Reveal”. After a short countdown, the bar on the screen shows how the room voted. Now you compare: does the audience majority match what the couple showed with their shoes?

round.title: Keep going.
round.text: With “Next question” you work through your questions.

tips: 10 to 15 questions is ideal. Enough tension without dragging.
Insider questions bring the loudest laughs. Mix classics with your personal couple knowledge.
Keep both devices plugged in so nothing dies mid-game.
Wait to close until enough guests have voted. You see the vote count live in the controls.
Have the couple raise their shoes first, then reveal. That's the moment of suspense.
Do a short dry run beforehand so everything's dialed in on the day.
guide.downloadHint: Got everything? Print the guide for the big day.
